nodes.title: Import ngModel for numeric controls
nodes.why: FormsModule is the only external template dependency; built-in @if and @for do not require CommonModule.
nodes.alternative: Keep CommonModule for control flow that Angular now provides directly.
nodes.tradeoff: Minimal imports stay clear, but any later CommonModule pipe must be added explicitly.

nodes.title: Render a grid only for positive dimensions
nodes.why: The branch prevents an empty table shell and gives zero dimensions a clear fallback state.
nodes.alternative: Always render table markup even when one dimension has no cells.
nodes.tradeoff: The conditional adds a separate empty state, but the visible result is easier to understand.

nodes.title: Track generated rows by stable numeric value

anchor.snippet: @for (row of rows; track row; let rowIndex = $index) {

nodes.why: The generated row values are unique and let Angular preserve row identity across dimension changes.
nodes.alternative: Track only by the current index without a stable row value.
nodes.tradeoff: Numeric identity is simple for this generated range, while richer row data would benefit from explicit ids.

nodes.title: Clamp requested dimensions
nodes.why: Bounding rows and columns prevents negative or unreasonably large grids from reaching the DOM.
nodes.alternative: Trust any numeric input and allocate arrays directly from it.
nodes.tradeoff: The cap limits the demo to twenty rows or columns, but it protects preview responsiveness.
